ReleaseLabel: dabbler-ai-orchestration 0.41.0 local VSIX (Set 087 'Module-Organized Projects - Foundations', Session 3 of 4; the version bump happens at release, after Session 4)
Notes: Ad-hoc per-session UAT for Session 3: the 'New module' scaffold (a docs/modules.yaml entry + a plan stub), module-targeted plan authoring / decomposition (the flows ask WHICH module and stamp 'module: <slug>' into generated specs), and the CODEOWNERS + monorepo-CI teaching templates new projects now scaffold with. Sessions 1-2 (the manifest reader and the Explorer's module grouping) were verified earlier under the operator-armed E2E gate and are NOT re-walked here. Every step below was ALREADY exercised for real before this checklist was offered to you: the full extension unit suite (1,346 passing / 0 failing, incl. 33 new Set-087-S3 tests) plus a live end-to-end dogfood run on 2026-07-10 that drove the real new-module flow, the real module picker, and the real prompt builders against a scratch repo (12/12 PASS; the exact strings quoted in each Expectation are from that run and from current source). What automation genuinely cannot check - and what this walk is FOR - is the real VS Code UI surface: input boxes appearing with the right copy, the QuickPick, toasts, and the button in the Getting Started form. UAT runs against a LOCALLY BUILT VSIX ONLY: the orchestrator built one for you during authoring at tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.41.0.vsix (it contains the Set 087 S1+S2+S3 changes). Install it via Extensions > '...' menu > 'Install from VSIX...', then reload when prompted. Walks 1-5 all happen in ONE fresh empty folder (call it uat-modules); walk 6 uses a second fresh folder (uat-single); walks 7-8 use a third fresh folder (uat-scaffold). Walk 7 chooses a $0 budget with 'Skip verification' ON PURPOSE so the walk spends nothing.

Review.Subarea: Walk 1 - New module from the Getting Started form
Review.ItemLabel: The step-2 'New module...' button scaffolds a manifest entry + plan stub and opens the stub
Review.HumanAction:
1. Create a new EMPTY folder anywhere (e.g. C:\temp\uat-modules) and open it in VS Code (File > Open Folder...).
2. Click the Dabbler AI Orchestration icon in the Activity Bar. The Getting Started form appears.
3. In step '2. Create or import a project plan', find the button labeled exactly 'New module...' (after 'Copy prompt for planning'). Hover it for ~2 seconds and read the tooltip.
4. Click 'New module...'. An input box titled 'New module (1/2): slug' appears with placeholder 'greeter'. Type exactly: greeter and press Enter.
5. A second input box titled 'New module (2/2): display title' appears. Type exactly: Greeter and press Enter.
Review.Expectation: The hover tooltip in step 3 begins 'Optional, for teams: declare a module in docs/modules.yaml'. After step 5: (a) an editor tab opens on docs/modules/greeter/project-plan.md whose first line is exactly '# Greeter — module project plan'; (b) a toast reads exactly: Module "greeter" declared in a new docs/modules.yaml. Plan stub created at docs/modules/greeter/project-plan.md — fill it in, then decompose it into session sets. (c) the file docs/modules.yaml exists and starts with a comment block headed '# docs/modules.yaml — the module manifest' followed by 'modules:' and one entry with 'slug: greeter', 'title: "Greeter"', 'codeRoots: []' and 'planPath: docs/modules/greeter/project-plan.md'.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: moduleAuthoring.test.ts > 'runNewModuleFlow' happy-path + 'scaffoldNewModule' fresh-manifest tests (real fs); AI exploratory check 2026-07-10: drove the real flow against a scratch repo (Walk A of the dogfood run, PASS). gettingStartedHtml.test.ts pins the button + hover copy.

Review.Subarea: Walk 2 - slug validation is live and fail-loud
Review.ItemLabel: Bad slugs are rejected inline with specific messages; Esc writes nothing
Review.HumanAction:
Still in uat-modules: click 'New module...' again.
1. In the slug box type exactly: Greeter (capital G) and read the inline validation message under the box.
2. Clear it and type exactly: greeter (the module you just made) and read the message.
3. Press Esc to cancel.
4. Open docs/modules.yaml and count the entries.
Review.Expectation: Step 1 shows exactly: Module slugs are kebab-case: lowercase letters and digits, joined by single hyphens (e.g. "greeter", "payment-api"). Step 2 shows exactly: Module "greeter" already exists in docs/modules.yaml. While either message shows, Enter does not accept the value. After Esc (step 3), docs/modules.yaml still has exactly ONE entry (greeter) - nothing was written.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: moduleAuthoring.test.ts > 'slug validation' matrix (exact messages) + 'Esc on either input box cancels without writing'.

Review.Subarea: Walk 3 - second module via the Command Palette
Review.ItemLabel: 'Dabbler: New Module' appends to the existing manifest without touching its text
Review.HumanAction:
Still in uat-modules:
1. Open docs/modules.yaml and add a comment line anywhere above the 'modules:' line, e.g.: # my note. Save.
2. Ctrl+Shift+P > type 'Dabbler: New Module' > Enter.
3. Slug: clock (Enter). Title: press Enter WITHOUT typing anything (empty = default).
4. Re-open docs/modules.yaml.
Review.Expectation: The toast reads exactly: Module "clock" appended to docs/modules.yaml. Plan stub created at docs/modules/clock/project-plan.md — fill it in, then decompose it into session sets. In docs/modules.yaml: your '# my note' comment line is STILL THERE untouched, the greeter entry is byte-identical to before, and a new second entry 'slug: clock' with 'title: "clock"' (the empty title defaulted to the slug) sits at the end. docs/modules/clock/project-plan.md exists and opened in the editor.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: moduleAuthoring.test.ts > 'append preserves the existing file's text — comments and formatting included' + 'empty title defaults to the slug'; dogfood Walk A scaffolded exactly this greeter-then-clock sequence (PASS).

Review.Subarea: Walk 4 - the decomposition prompt asks which module and stamps it
Review.ItemLabel: With two modules, 'Copy prompt to build session sets' shows a module QuickPick and the copied prompt is module-stamped
Review.HumanAction:
Still in uat-modules (two modules exist):
1. In the Getting Started form's step '3. Build session sets', click 'Copy prompt to build session sets'.
2. A QuickPick appears with placeholder 'Which module is this for?'. It lists 'Greeter' (with 'greeter' beside it) then 'clock', each showing a 'plan: ...' detail line. Click 'clock'.
3. After the 'Session-set generation prompt copied to clipboard...' toast, open a new untitled editor (Ctrl+N) and paste (Ctrl+V).
4. In the pasted text, use Ctrl+F to find: module: clock — then find: docs/modules/clock/project-plan.md — then find: docs/planning/project-plan.md
Review.Expectation: The QuickPick rows appear in manifest order (Greeter first). In the pasted prompt: (a) the worked example's 'Session Set Configuration' yaml block contains the line 'module: clock' directly under the 'tier:' line; (b) a hard-requirements bullet begins '- **Module:** declare `module: clock` in EVERY generated set's'; (c) a guidance bullet begins '- **Module.** This decomposition targets the **clock** module' and contains 'Recommended (not enforced)'; (d) the closing section 'The project plan (read it from the workspace)' names docs/modules/clock/project-plan.md; (e) the search for docs/planning/project-plan.md finds NOTHING - the module's own plan replaced it.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: sessionGenPrompt.test.ts > 'buildSessionGenPrompt — module targeting (Set 087 S3)' (all three tests); consumerBootstrap.test.ts > 'module context renders the module: line'; dogfood Walk C rendered this exact prompt from the canonical bundle (PASS).

Review.Subarea: Walk 5 - the planning prompt targets the picked module's plan
Review.ItemLabel: 'Copy prompt for planning' asks which module and names the module plan path
Review.HumanAction:
Still in uat-modules:
1. In step 2 of the form, click 'Copy prompt for planning'.
2. In the 'Which module is this for?' QuickPick, click 'Greeter'.
3. Read the toast, then paste the clipboard into an untitled editor.
Review.Expectation: The toast reads exactly: Plan-authoring prompt copied to clipboard. Paste it into your AI assistant, then save the result as docs/modules/greeter/project-plan.md — or import it with 'Import project-plan.md'. The pasted prompt asks for a plan 'for the "Greeter" module (`greeter`) of my software project', says the plan 'covers ONLY the greeter module', and ends 'Format as a clean Markdown document I can save as docs/modules/greeter/project-plan.md.'
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: gettingStartedActions.test.ts > 'planImport — module-aware targeting (Set 087 S3)' (copyPlanningPrompt tests); dogfood Walk D built this exact prompt (PASS).

Review.Subarea: Walk 6 - a single-module repo auto-selects with a visible notice, no picker
Review.ItemLabel: One module in the manifest: the flows never show a QuickPick but always SAY which module they targeted
Review.HumanAction:
1. Create a second EMPTY folder (e.g. C:\temp\uat-single) and open it in VS Code.
2. Ctrl+Shift+P > 'Dabbler: New Module' > slug: greeter > title: Greeter.
3. In the Getting Started form's step 2, click 'Copy prompt for planning'.
Review.Expectation: Step 3 shows NO QuickPick. Two toasts appear: first exactly 'Using module "Greeter" (greeter) — the only module in docs/modules.yaml.' and then the 'Plan-authoring prompt copied to clipboard...' toast naming docs/modules/greeter/project-plan.md as the save path.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: moduleAuthoring.test.ts > 'pickModuleForAuthoring: one module auto-selects WITH the operator notice (ruling Q2)'; gettingStartedActions.test.ts > 'copyPlanningPrompt: single module auto-targets its plan with the notice'.

Review.Subarea: Walk 7 - a repo with NO modules.yaml behaves exactly as before
Review.ItemLabel: No manifest: no module picker anywhere, repo-level plan path unchanged
Review.HumanAction:
1. Create a third EMPTY folder (e.g. C:\temp\uat-scaffold) and open it in VS Code (do NOT create any module).
2. In the Getting Started form's step 2, click 'Copy prompt for planning'.
Review.Expectation: NO QuickPick and NO 'Using module...' toast appear - just the single toast: Plan-authoring prompt copied to clipboard. Paste it into your AI assistant, then save the result as docs/planning/project-plan.md — or import it with 'Import project-plan.md'. (The pre-087 behavior, byte-identical prompt.)
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: gettingStartedActions.test.ts > 'copyPlanningPrompt copies the authoring prompt and toasts' (the Set 060 suite runs with no manifest); planImport.buildPlanningPrompt renders the repo-level prompt byte-identical (pinned by the same suite); dogfood confirmed pickModuleForAuthoring returns 'none' with zero UI on a manifest-less root.

Review.Subarea: Walk 8 - 'Build project structure' scaffolds CODEOWNERS + monorepo CI
Review.ItemLabel: A new project starts with .github/CODEOWNERS and .github/workflows/monorepo-ci.yml teaching templates
Review.HumanAction:
Still in uat-scaffold (from walk 7):
1. In step 1 of the form, leave the 'Full' radio and 'Direct provider API keys' selected. In 'Verification budget (USD, not-to-exceed)' type exactly: 0
2. In the choice that appears below, select 'Skip verification' (this walk must spend nothing).
3. Click 'Build project structure' and wait for the build to finish (the venv install can take a minute or two).
4. When the summary toast appears, open the Explorer file tree and inspect the .github folder.
Review.Expectation: The toast begins 'Project structure built (full tier):' and counts the files written. The folder now contains .github/CODEOWNERS and .github/workflows/monorepo-ci.yml. CODEOWNERS is ENTIRELY comment lines (every non-blank line starts with '#') and includes the worked Priya/Sam/Alex example plus a comment block explaining the integration `touches` review rule. monorepo-ci.yml declares 'name: Monorepo CI', triggers on push to main + pull_request, and its only ACTIVE job is 'all-modules' whose step is an echo placeholder that says to replace it - committing these files as-is cannot break a build or assign a reviewer.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: gettingStartedActions.test.ts > 'writes exactly the structure artifacts and NO starter session set' (asserts both .github paths); gitScaffoldCore.test.ts full-render tests; coldStartSnapshot.test.ts golden includes both files for both tiers; the monorepo-ci template is valid YAML (loaded + parsed by the bundle tests).
